scriptName: VS Code Folder + Background Context Menu Repair
description: Re-registers VS Code right-click entries for folders AND empty-space (background) and removes only the file-target leaf, then restarts Explorer.

messages.scriptDisabled: Folder + background repair is disabled in config.
messages.notAdmin: This script must be run as Administrator.
messages.removingTarget: Removing context menu entry on target '{target}': {path}
messages.restartingExplorer: Restarting explorer.exe so context menu refreshes...
messages.unknownEdition: Edition '{name}' not found in config.editions -- skipping
messages.completedWithWarnings: Folder + background repair completed with warnings -- review log above.
messages.preCheckHeader: Pre-check: inspecting current registry state (no writes).
messages.preCheckDryRunDone: Dry-run complete -- no changes applied. Re-run without 'dry-run' / 'precheck' to apply.

commands.Name: repair
commands.Description: Default. Apply fix for both editions and restart Explorer.

commands.Name: repair-vscode
commands.Description: Transactional: precheck -> backup -> apply -> verify -> auto-rollback on failure.

commands.Name: dry-run
commands.Description: Preview planned changes (also: precheck, plan, whatif). NO admin, NO writes.

commands.Name: verify
commands.Description: Re-run the PASS/FAIL verification table only. NO writes.

commands.Name: rollback
commands.Description: Restore the most recent .reg snapshot per edition (also: --Rollback flag).

commands.Name: no-restart
commands.Description: Apply the fix but skip the explorer.exe restart.

parameters.Name: -Edition
parameters.Description: Limit to one edition: 'stable' or 'insiders'. Default: both, auto-detected.

parameters.Name: -Rollback
parameters.Description: Restore latest snapshot for each detected edition (or use -BackupFile).

parameters.Name: -BackupFile
parameters.Description: Explicit .reg snapshot path to restore (overrides 'pick latest').

examples: .\run.ps1 dry-run                         # preview only, no admin needed
.\run.ps1 repair-vscode -Edition stable   # transactional repair for VS Code Stable only
.\run.ps1 verify                          # re-run PASS/FAIL verification table only

permissions.title: Required permissions
lines: Administrator (elevated PowerShell) is required for any WRITE command:
repair, repair-vscode, rollback, no-restart.
Reason: writes target HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\Directory\shell\VSCode and
HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\Directory\Background\shell\VSCode (machine-wide).
NO admin required for read-only commands:
dry-run, precheck, plan, whatif, verify, --Help.
On non-elevated runs of write commands the script fails fast and prints a
copy-paste 'Start-Process pwsh -Verb RunAs ...' retry hint (no auto-UAC).

behavior.title: Expected behavior -- empty vs non-empty folders
lines: After a successful run, 'Open with Code' / 'Open with Code - Insiders' MUST
appear in BOTH right-click scenarios:
1. Right-click ON a folder icon (folder may be empty OR contain files).
Backed by: HKCR\Directory\shell\VSCode  (target = 'directory').
Action: opens that folder in VS Code (passes %V = clicked path).
2. Right-click on EMPTY space INSIDE an open folder window.
Backed by: HKCR\Directory\Background\shell\VSCode  (target = 'background').
Action: opens the CURRENT folder (the one you are looking at).
Works whether the folder is empty or full -- the 'background' target
is the part of the window NOT covered by any file/folder icon.
After a successful run, the entry MUST NOT appear when right-clicking a FILE.
Backed by removal of: HKCR\*\shell\VSCode  (target = 'file').
If a folder is so densely packed that no empty space is visible, scroll or
resize the window -- the 'background' entry only shows when you click
actual background pixels, not file rows.

verifySection.title: How to verify the fix
lines: 1. Automated (built-in): the script prints a colored PASS/FAIL summary
table after every run that maps each registry target to its real
right-click scenario. Re-run standalone any time:
.\run.ps1 verify
2. Manual smoke test in Windows Explorer:
a. Open any folder. Right-click a sub-folder icon  ->  'Open with Code' MUST be visible.
b. Open that sub-folder. Right-click on empty whitespace  ->  'Open with Code' MUST be visible.
c. Right-click any FILE in the folder  ->  'Open with Code' MUST NOT be visible.
3. From an elevated PowerShell, query the registry directly:
reg query 'HKCR\Directory\shell\VSCode\command' /ve
reg query 'HKCR\Directory\Background\shell\VSCode\command' /ve
reg query 'HKCR\*\shell\VSCode'                # MUST report 'unable to find'
The two 'present' commands should each end with: "<path-to-Code.exe>" "%V"
4. Inspect the per-run change ledger + JSON log:
.logs\registry-backups\script53-*.reg          (BEFORE snapshot)
.logs\registry-backups\script53-*.json         (per-key change rows)
.logs\vs-code-folder-+-background-context-menu-repair.json
